title: Cybersecurity Analyst

rationales.taskAutomation: AI SOC copilots now triage alerts, correlate logs, and draft compliance reports end-to-end; novel-threat judgment and incident-response ownership still require an analyst.
rationales.toolMaturity: Vendor SOC copilots (Microsoft Security Copilot, CrowdStrike Charlotte AI) are credible and improving fast, but not yet as embedded as coding copilots.
rationales.adoption: Large enterprises and tech-forward security teams run AI triage in production; mid-market and regulated sectors lag.
rationales.agenticExposure: Agents can autonomously run tier-1 alert triage and vulnerability scan sweeps; threat hunting and incident command stay human-led.

tasksAtRisk: Tier-1 SOC alert triage and correlation
Routine vulnerability scanning and patch-priority reports
Compliance report drafting from scan output
tasksGrowing: Threat hunting for novel, non-signature attack vectors
AI system security -- prompt injection, model poisoning, data exfiltration defense
Incident response leadership and digital forensics
salarySource: BLS OES May 2024, SOC 15-1212 (Information Security Analysts), p25-p75
insight: AI SOC copilots now clear most tier-1 alert volume, but they also expand the attack surface faster than they close it. Analysts who supervise agentic triage and specialize in AI-native threats -- prompt injection, model poisoning -- are pulling ahead of generalists.
